tools.name: policy_preflight
tools.description: Pre-flight security check before any tool call. Evaluates all policies, computes risk score, checks rate limits, and returns allow/deny/require_approval decision. Call this BEFORE executing any agent tool. Writes to audit log automatically.

tools.name: tool_risk_score
tools.description: Compute 0-100 risk score for any tool + input combination. 0=minimal risk (read-only), 100=critical (payment/irreversible). Detects secrets, injection attempts, high-value amounts. Use before deciding whether to proceed with a tool call.

tools.name: approval_required
tools.description: Check if a tool call requires human approval before execution. Returns requires_approval=true/false with matched policy list. Set register_pending=true to create a trackable approval request with an approval_url for human review.

tools.name: audit_log_write
tools.description: Write a tool call result to the persistent, signed audit log. Call this AFTER tool execution with the outcome. Each entry is cryptographically signed for tamper-evidence. Essential for compliance, DORA, MiCA audit trail requirements.

tools.name: audit_log_query
tools.description: Query the persistent audit trail. Filter by agent, tool, decision, time range. Returns signed entries with tamper-detection. Use for compliance reporting, anomaly detection, or agent behaviour analysis.

tools.name: decision_explain
tools.description: Get a human-readable explanation of why a tool call was allowed or denied. Pass request_id from a previous policy_preflight for stored explanation, or provide tool_name + tool_args for fresh analysis. Explains matched policies, risk score breakdown, and recommendation.

tools.name: rate_limit_check
tools.description: Check if an agent has exceeded rate limits. Returns per-window usage (minute/hour/day) with percentage used. Limits: 200/min, 5000/hr, 50000/day per agent. Use before high-frequency tool calls or for agent health monitoring.

tools.name: payment_policy_check
tools.description: Validate a payment against policy rules before execution. Checks amount limits (>100k warns, >1M blocks), recipient allowlist/denylist, supported currencies/networks, AML reporting thresholds, and MiCA flags. Returns approved/rejected with full violation list and risk score.

tools.name: spend_limit_check
tools.description: Check if a payment amount stays within agent spend limits. Default limits: 10,000/call, 50,000/hr, 200,000/day. Trusted agents: 100,000/call, 500,000/hr, 2,000,000/day. Returns within_limits=true/false with headroom percentage.

tools.name: secret_exposure_check
tools.description: Deep scan any text/payload for secrets, credentials, and PII. Detects: API keys (OpenAI, GitHub, AWS), tokens (Slack, Bearer), private keys (ETH, Bitcoin), credentials (passwords, secrets), and PII (emails, credit cards, SSNs). Returns findings with severity and remediation guidance.

tools.name: payload_safety_check
tools.description: Comprehensive safety scan for injection attacks and dangerous patterns. Detects: prompt injection, jailbreak/DAN attempts, role hijacking, SQL injection (UNION/DROP/OR 1=1), XSS, Python/JS/Shell code injection, path traversal, oversized payloads, null bytes. Returns safe=true/false with finding list

tools.name: replay_guard_check
tools.description: Detect replay attacks — identical requests sent multiple times in a time window. Uses SHA256 fingerprint of (agent_id + tool_name + args). Default window: 300 seconds (5 min). Returns is_replay=true/false with duplicate count and first/last seen timestamps.

tools.name: cross_tool_anomaly_check
tools.description: Detect anomalous tool usage patterns across an agent recent history. Checks risky tool combinations (AML-then-payment, wallet-recon-then-transfer), high call frequency, repeated denials, broad reconnaissance, elevated risk scores.

tools.name: scope_check
tools.description: Check if agent has required scope for a tool. Roles: admin, compliance_officer, trader, auditor, developer, readonly. Returns has_scope + missing scope + granting roles.

tools.name: session_validate
tools.description: Create/validate/invalidate agent sessions with role, scopes, TTL and call budget. Actions: create|validate|invalidate|info.

tools.name: tenant_policy_check
tools.description: Multi-tenant governance. Tenants: default, fintech_eu (MiCA/DORA), defi_protocol, enterprise_read. Checks tool blocklists, max risk scores, spend limits. Actions: check|list.

tools.name: threat_intel_check
tools.description: Check entity against threat intelligence. Auto-detects ETH addresses, IPs, domains. Checks sanctions (Tornado Cash), disposable services, behavioral analysis from audit log. Returns threat_level: none|low|medium|high|critical.

tools.name: output_safety_scan
tools.description: Post-execution output scanner. Checks tool output for PII leaks (email, phone, SSN, IBAN), secret exposure, data exfiltration patterns (outbound URLs, base64), and tool poisoning (injected instructions). Verdict: clean|warn|flag|block.

tools.name: emergency_kill
tools.description: Emergency kill-switch. Immediately terminates agent session(s), revokes pending approvals, blocks rate limits, and audit-logs the emergency. Use for: compromised agents, runaway automation, suspicious behavior. kill_type: full|session_only|soft.

tools.name: tool_manifest_verify
tools.description: Supply-chain verification for MCP tools. Checks publisher identity against allowlist, scans tool descriptions for prompt injection, validates server domain and signing capability. Verdict: trusted|caution|block.

tools.name: approval_resolve
tools.description: Resolve a pending approval request. Approve or deny a tool call that was flagged for human review. Updates agent state and creates audit trail. Requires authentication.

tools.name: approval_list
tools.description: List approval requests. Filter by status (pending/approved/denied/all), agent_id, or tool_name. Shows summary stats.

tools.name: policy_register
tools.description: View the central policy registry. Query tiers (T1-T4), tool classifications, escalation rules. Actions: summary (default), lookup (by tool_name), tiers, rules, tools (by tier_id).

tools.name: guard_metrics
tools.description: AgentGuard operational metrics. Returns decision stats, top agents/tools, risk distribution, daily activity, approval stats, and tier distribution.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "tool_risk_score" references sensitive artifacts (secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Compute 0-100 risk score for any tool + input combination. 0=minimal risk (read-only), 100=critical (payment/irreversible). Detects secrets, injection attempts,…

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "secret_exposure_check" references sensitive artifacts (private key, credentials, password, secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "output_safety_scan" references sensitive artifacts (secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Post-execution output scanner. Checks tool output for PII leaks (email, phone, SSN, IBAN), secret exposure, data exfiltration patterns (outbound URLs, base64), …

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: approval_required, secret_exposure_check · Private data: tool_risk_score, secret_exposure_check, cross_tool_anomaly_check, output_safety_scan · Public sink: output_safety_scan · Destructive: payload_safety_check, emergency_kill.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: approval_required, secret_exposure_check · Private data: tool_risk_score, secret_exposure_check, cross_tool_anomaly_check, output_safety_scan · Public sink: output_safety_scan · Destructive: payload_safety_check, emergency_kill

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
